reasoning: The FRED trend model gives a baseline probability of an increase of ~65% based on the upward trend since February. However, short-term (1-week) movements in corporate bond spreads largely behave as a random walk, with a true base rate closer to 50%. Over the last five days of available data (March 9 to March 13), the spread actually decreased from 3.66 to 3.57, countering the longer-term upward trend. A 1-week change in a highly volatile financial time series is close to a coin flip, so I am adjusting the 65% statistical anchor toward 50% to account for short-term mean reversion and the recent downward correction.
